proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_enzalutamide have higher mean pfs_months than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_abiraterone have higher mean pfs_months than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_docetaxel have higher mean pfs_months than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have higher mean pfs_months than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_lu177_psma have higher mean pfs_months than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have higher mean pfs_months than those not receiving it.

analyses.result_summary: PFS by treatment_enzalutamide: mean(treatment_enzalutamide=1)=3.728 vs mean=0=3.755, diff=-0.0263, t=-1.428, p=0.153 (n1=20076, n0=29924)
PFS by treatment_abiraterone: mean(treatment_abiraterone=1)=3.740 vs mean=0=3.746, diff=-0.0056, t=-0.285, p=0.776 (n1=14991, n0=35009)
PFS by treatment_docetaxel: mean(treatment_docetaxel=1)=3.732 vs mean=0=3.749, diff=-0.0172, t=-0.876, p=0.381 (n1=15187, n0=34813)
PFS by treatment_olaparib: mean(treatment_olaparib=1)=3.829 vs mean=0=3.735, diff=+0.0943, t=2.940, p=0.00329 (n1=5098, n0=44902)
PFS by treatment_lu177_psma: mean(treatment_lu177_psma=1)=3.741 vs mean=0=3.745, diff=-0.0041, t=-0.161, p=0.872 (n1=7504, n0=42496)
PFS by treatment_pembrolizumab: mean(treatment_pembrolizumab=1)=3.805 vs mean=0=3.741, diff=+0.0638, t=1.488, p=0.137 (n1=2384, n0=47616)

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with mcrpc=1 (castration-resistant) have lower mean pfs_months than those with mcrpc=0.
Patients with visceral_mets=1 have lower mean pfs_months than those with visceral_mets=0.
Patients with liver_mets=1 have lower mean pfs_months than those with liver_mets=0.
Patients with bone_mets=1 have lower mean pfs_months than those with bone_mets=0.
Higher ecog_ps is correlated with lower pfs_months (Pearson r negative).
Higher gleason_score is correlated with lower pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: PFS by visceral_mets: mean(visceral_mets=1)=3.706 vs mean=0=3.754, diff=-0.0477, t=-2.114, p=0.0345 (n1=9973, n0=40027)
PFS by liver_mets: mean(liver_mets=1)=3.743 vs mean=0=3.744, diff=-0.0017, t=-0.065, p=0.948 (n1=7392, n0=42608)

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher psa_ng_ml is correlated with lower pfs_months.
Higher ldh_u_l is correlated with lower pfs_months.
Higher albumin_g_dl is correlated with higher pfs_months.
Higher alkaline_phosphatase_u_l is correlated with lower pfs_months.
Higher hemoglobin_g_dl is correlated with higher pfs_months.
Higher crp_mg_l is correlated with lower pfs_months.
Higher nlr is correlated with lower pfs_months.
Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is correlated with lower pfs_months.
Older age_years is correlated with pfs_months (direction unspecified, suspect lower).
Mean pfs_months differs across race_ethnicity categories.
Mean pfs_months differs across insurance_type categories.
Patients with rural_residence=1 have different mean pfs_months than those with rural_residence=0.
Higher education_years is correlated with higher pfs_months.
Higher smoking_pack_years is correlated with lower pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: PFS across race_ethnicity (ANOVA): F=0.564, p=0.689; means={'asian': 3.797, 'black': 3.741, 'hispanic': 3.737, 'other': 3.749, 'white': 3.741}

proposed_hypotheses.text: Olaparib has a larger PFS benefit in brca2_mutation=1 patients than brca2_mutation=0 (positive treatment_olaparib×brca2_mutation interaction).
Pembrolizumab has a larger PFS benefit in msi_high=1 patients than msi_high=0 (positive treatment_pembrolizumab×msi_high interaction).
Lu177-PSMA has a larger PFS benefit in psma_high=1 patients than psma_high=0 (positive treatment_lu177_psma×psma_high interaction).
Enzalutamide has a smaller PFS benefit in ar_v7_positive=1 patients than ar_v7_positive=0 (negative treatment_enzalutamide×ar_v7_positive interaction).
Abiraterone has a smaller PFS benefit in ar_v7_positive=1 patients than ar_v7_positive=0 (negative treatment_abiraterone×ar_v7_positive interaction).

analyses.code: OLS(pfs ~ treatment_olaparib + brca2_mutation + treatment_olaparib:brca2_mutation)  # interaction term
OLS(pfs ~ treatment_pembrolizumab + msi_high + treatment_pembrolizumab:msi_high)  # interaction term
OLS(pfs ~ treatment_lu177_psma + psma_high + treatment_lu177_psma:psma_high)  # interaction term
OLS(pfs ~ treatment_enzalutamide + ar_v7_positive + treatment_enzalutamide:ar_v7_positive)  # interaction term
OLS(pfs ~ treatment_abiraterone + ar_v7_positive + treatment_abiraterone:ar_v7_positive)  # interaction term

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within brca2_mutation=1, patients on treatment_olaparib have higher mean pfs_months than those off treatment_olaparib.
Within brca2_mutation=0, treatment_olaparib does NOT improve mean pfs_months (refined null).
Within msi_high=1, treatment_pembrolizumab improves mean pfs_months.
Within psma_high=1, treatment_lu177_psma improves mean pfs_months.
Within ar_v7_positive=1, treatment_enzalutamide does NOT improve mean pfs_months (refined null).

analyses.result_summary: olaparib in BRCA2+: among brca2_mutation=1 (n=4996): mean(treatment_olaparib=1)=5.216 vs =3.671, diff=+1.5455, t=11.353, p=3.95e-27
olaparib in BRCA2-: among brca2_mutation=0 (n=45004): mean(treatment_olaparib=1)=3.668 vs =3.742, diff=-0.0734, t=-2.344, p=0.0191
pembrolizumab in MSI-high: among msi_high=1 (n=1528): mean(treatment_pembrolizumab=1)=3.527 vs =3.792, diff=-0.2652, t=-1.187, p=0.239
lu177_psma in PSMA-high: among psma_high=1 (n=29962): mean(treatment_lu177_psma=1)=3.743 vs =3.754, diff=-0.0110, t=-0.337, p=0.736
enzalutamide in AR-V7+: among ar_v7_positive=1 (n=10038): mean(treatment_enzalutamide=1)=3.719 vs =3.744, diff=-0.0244, t=-0.588, p=0.557

In a multivariable OLS adjusting for age_years, ecog_ps, mcrpc, visceral_mets, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, psa_ng_ml, weight_loss_pct_6mo, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, hemoglobin_g_dl, the coefficient on ecog_ps remains negative and significant.
In the same multivariable OLS, the coefficient on albumin_g_dl remains positive and significant.
In the same multivariable OLS, the coefficient on mcrpc remains negative and significant.
In the same multivariable OLS, the coefficient on age_years remains positive and significant (older = longer pfs in this synthetic cohort).

analyses.result_summary: MV PFS regression: adj-coef(ecog_ps)=-1.1510, p=0, R²=0.9465, n=50000
MV PFS regression: adj-coef(albumin_g_dl)=+0.4785, p=0, R²=0.9465, n=50000
MV PFS regression: adj-coef(mcrpc)=-0.3757, p=0, R²=0.9465, n=50000
MV PFS regression: adj-coef(age_years)=+0.1737, p=0, R²=0.9465, n=50000

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within mcrpc=1 patients, olaparib has a larger PFS benefit in brca2_mutation=1 than brca2_mutation=0.
Within mcrpc=0 patients, olaparib has a larger PFS benefit in brca2_mutation=1 than brca2_mutation=0.
Within visceral_mets=1, treatment_docetaxel improves PFS more than in visceral_mets=0 (positive interaction).

analyses.code: OLS in mcrpc=1: pfs ~ treatment_olaparib + brca2_mutation + treatment_olaparib:brca2_mutation
analyses.result_summary: olaparib×brca2 in mCRPC: in mcrpc=1 (n=27481), interaction β=+1.6300, p=6.02e-36

analyses.code: OLS in mcrpc=0: pfs ~ treatment_olaparib + brca2_mutation + treatment_olaparib:brca2_mutation
analyses.result_summary: olaparib×brca2 in non-mCRPC: in mcrpc=0 (n=22519), interaction β=+1.6267, p=1.33e-28

analyses.code: OLS(pfs ~ treatment_docetaxel + visceral_mets + treatment_docetaxel:visceral_mets)  # interaction term

proposed_hypotheses.text: The PFS benefit of treatment_docetaxel decreases as ecog_ps increases (negative treatment_docetaxel×ecog_ps interaction).
The PFS benefit of treatment_olaparib does not depend on ecog_ps (interaction null).
The PFS benefit of treatment_enzalutamide decreases as ecog_ps increases.

analyses.code: OLS(pfs ~ treatment_docetaxel + ecog_ps + treatment_docetaxel:ecog_ps)  # interaction term
OLS(pfs ~ treatment_olaparib + ecog_ps + treatment_olaparib:ecog_ps)  # interaction term
OLS(pfs ~ treatment_enzalutamide + ecog_ps + treatment_enzalutamide:ecog_ps)  # interaction term

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within brca2_mutation=1 patients, the adjusted coefficient of treatment_olaparib on pfs_months remains positive and significant after controlling for ecog_ps, mcrpc, age_years, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, weight_loss_pct_6mo.
Within brca2_mutation=0 patients, the adjusted coefficient of treatment_olaparib on pfs_months is null after controlling for the same prognostic confounders.

analyses.result_summary: adj olaparib in BRCA2+: in brca2_mutation=1 (n=4996), adj-β(treatment_olaparib)=+1.4317, p=8.58e-273
adj olaparib in BRCA2-: in brca2_mutation=0 (n=45004), adj-β(treatment_olaparib)=-0.0026, p=0.659

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with any matched_therapy (olaparib+BRCA2 OR pembrolizumab+MSI-high OR Lu177+PSMA-high) have higher mean pfs_months than those with no matched therapy.
Adjusted for prognostic covariates (age, ECOG, mCRPC, albumin, LDH, weight_loss), the matched_therapy indicator remains a positive predictor of pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Matched vs unmatched: mean(matched≥1)=3.873 (n=5046) vs mean=0=3.730 (n=44954), diff=+0.1432, p=7.78e-06
adjusted matched_therapy effect: adj-coef(matched_therapy)=+0.1607, p=4.31e-97, R²=0.9330, n=50000

proposed_hypotheses.text: The PFS effect of treatment_enzalutamide is augmented when patients are also on treatment_abiraterone (positive enzalutamide×abiraterone interaction).
The PFS effect of treatment_docetaxel is augmented when patients are also on treatment_enzalutamide.
The PFS effect of treatment_olaparib in BRCA2+ patients is further augmented by concurrent treatment_abiraterone (3-way interaction not directly tested; instead test olaparib×abiraterone in BRCA2+).

analyses.code: OLS in brca2_mutation=1: pfs ~ treatment_olaparib + treatment_abiraterone + treatment_olaparib:treatment_abiraterone
analyses.result_summary: olaparib×abiraterone in BRCA2+: in brca2_mutation=1 (n=4996), interaction β=-0.1647, p=0.432

proposed_hypotheses.text: The PFS benefit of treatment_olaparib varies with age_years (treatment_olaparib×age_years interaction nonzero).
The PFS benefit of treatment_docetaxel varies with age_years (negative interaction expected).
In a quadratic model age_years + age_years², pfs_months has a non-monotonic relationship with age (significant quadratic term).

analyses.code: OLS(pfs ~ treatment_olaparib + age_years + treatment_olaparib:age_years)  # interaction term
OLS(pfs ~ treatment_docetaxel + age_years + treatment_docetaxel:age_years)  # interaction term

analyses.result_summary: Quadratic age model: β(age²)=+0.000776, p=1.86e-112, R²=0.7346

proposed_hypotheses.text: In mcrpc=1 patients, mean pfs_months is lower for ar_v7_positive=1 vs 0.
In mcrpc=1 patients, mean pfs_months is higher for psma_high=1 vs 0.
In mcrpc=0 patients, ar_v7_positive does not affect mean pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: ar_v7 in mCRPC: among mcrpc=1 (n=27481): mean(ar_v7_positive=1)=3.486 vs =3.515, diff=-0.0293, t=-0.971, p=0.331
psma_high in mCRPC: among mcrpc=1 (n=27481): mean(psma_high=1)=3.520 vs =3.494, diff=+0.0266, t=1.082, p=0.279
ar_v7 in non-mCRPC: among mcrpc=0 (n=22519): mean(ar_v7_positive=1)=4.037 vs =4.029, diff=+0.0082, t=0.242, p=0.809

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a univariate model age_years vs pfs_months, the slope coefficient is positive and large in magnitude.
In a univariate model ecog_ps vs pfs_months, the slope coefficient is negative.
In a univariate model albumin_g_dl vs pfs_months, the slope is positive.
In a univariate model weight_loss_pct_6mo vs pfs_months, the slope is negative.
After adjusting for age_years, ecog_ps, mcrpc, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, weight_loss_pct_6mo, the rural_residence indicator does not predict pfs_months.
After adjustment, education_years does not predict pfs_months.
After adjustment, smoking_pack_years does not predict pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: adjusted rural_residence: adj-coef(rural_residence)=-0.0132, p=0.0256, R²=0.9325, n=50000
adjusted education_years: adj-coef(education_years)=-0.0003, p=0.749, R²=0.9325, n=50000
adjusted smoking_pack_years: adj-coef(smoking_pack_years)=-0.0000, p=0.679, R²=0.9325, n=50000

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable OLS combining the top significant prognostic predictors (age_years, ecog_ps, mcrpc, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, psa_ng_ml, weight_loss_pct_6mo, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, hemoglobin_g_dl) plus a matched_therapy indicator and the olaparib×brca2_mutation interaction explains substantially more variance than prognostic features alone (matched_therapy coefficient positive and significant).
In the final model, the olaparib×brca2_mutation interaction term is positive and significant.

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ prognostic + matched_therapy + olaparib + brca2 + olaparib*brca2
analyses.result_summary: Final MV model R²=0.9518, n=50000; β(matched_therapy)=+0.0036, p=0.606
Final MV model β(olaparib×brca2)=+1.4341, p=0, R²=0.9518
